articles.title: Israel and Hezbollah renew ceasefire after flare-up, but IDF to stay in southern Lebanon
articles.summary: Israel and Hezbollah agreed to a renewed ceasefire taking effect at 4 p.m. local time Friday, brokered by the US and Qatar after the deadliest flare-up since the US-Iran memorandum of understanding. Israel insists the IDF retains 'full freedom of action' and will not withdraw from southern Lebanon until security is restored.

articles.title: Lebanon says at least 47 killed in Israeli strikes as fighting peaks before truce
articles.summary: Lebanon's health ministry reported at least 47 people killed and 97 wounded in Israeli airstrikes and bombardment Friday, the deadliest day since the US-Iran deal. Israel said it struck about 150 Hezbollah targets and killed dozens of operatives in southern Lebanon.

articles.title: Four Israeli soldiers killed in Hezbollah attack in southern Lebanon; Netanyahu vows 'heavy price'
articles.summary: A Hezbollah strike on a tank killed four IDF soldiers, including 32-year-old Lt.-Col. Dor Gedalia Ben Simhon, commander of the 52nd Battalion, 401st Brigade. It was Israel's first combat death in Lebanon since the US-Iran deal. Netanyahu ordered the IDF to strike Hezbollah 'with full force.'

articles.title: Israel plans expanded occupation zone in Lebanon in defiance of US-Iran pact
articles.summary: The Israeli army published a map of a new, enlarged zone of control in southern Lebanon, signalling a significant expansion of its occupation and reserving the right to strike beyond occupation lines, challenging the recently signed Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding.

articles.title: Despite deal with US, Iran says ships must request Strait of Hormuz transit 48 hours in advance
articles.summary: Iran said vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz must submit requests 48 hours in advance, a caveat that complicates the reopening of the waterway envisaged under the US-Iran memorandum of understanding signed earlier in the week.

articles.title: US military says it has lifted naval blockade of Iranian ports
articles.summary: US Central Command announced it had lifted its naval blockade of Iranian ports, an early step in implementing the memorandum of understanding aimed at winding down the war that began in late February.

articles.title: US to release $12bn of Iran's frozen assets, state media reports
articles.summary: Under the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding, the US is to unfreeze $24bn in Iranian funds during a 60-day negotiating period, with $12bn made available before talks begin, Iranian state media reported.

articles.title: Iran says talks underway to reschedule postponed Switzerland meeting with US
articles.summary: Iran said a planned meeting with US officials in Switzerland had been postponed but arrangements were underway for talks in the coming days, adding the meeting was no longer urgent because the memorandum of understanding had already been signed digitally.

articles.title: Gaza ceasefire 'failing' as hunger, rats and rubble define daily life, UN humanitarian chief warns
articles.summary: The UN humanitarian chief told the Security Council that the Gaza ceasefire is failing, with humanitarian recovery dangerously incomplete seven months after Resolution 2803 backed the US peace plan, and demanded 'dignity' for Gaza's population.

articles.title: Saudi envoy tells UN Security Council two-state solution only path to lasting Mideast peace
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia's permanent representative told the Security Council that the Palestinian issue remains at the heart of the conflict, warning genuine peace cannot be achieved without ending Israel's occupation and implementing a two-state solution.

articles.title: IRGC sets up secretive cells in Iraq to conduct drone attacks on Gulf, Reuters reports
articles.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guard has established covert cells of elite Iraqi Shiite fighters near Basra and Samawa that have launched at least seven drone attacks toward the Gulf, according to a Reuters report cited by Arab News.

articles.title: Extremist Israeli ministers call for 'burning' Lebanon, opening 'gates of hell' after soldiers killed
articles.summary: Far-right Israeli ministers called for escalating attacks on Lebanon and 'opening the gates of hell' following the deaths of four IDF soldiers, drawing condemnation as the renewed ceasefire took hold.

articles.title: Lebanese president Aoun condemns Israeli attacks, urges 'comprehensive ceasefire'
articles.summary: President Joseph Aoun condemned Israel's expanded attacks in southern and eastern Lebanon, which he said caused more killing and destruction, and called for a comprehensive ceasefire as fast as possible.

articles.title: Syria's wheat harvest expected to more than double this year
articles.summary: Syria's wheat production is forecast to more than double in 2026, helped by heavier rains and the state's recapture of a key northeastern growing area, easing pressure on the country's food supply.
